title: Wilfred
content:
혻혻혻혻What of these tender feet
혻혻혻혻That have never toddled yet?
혻혻혻혻What dances shall they beat,
혻혻혻혻With what red vintage wet?
혻혻혻혻In what wild way will they march or stray, by what sly paynims met?

혻혻혻혻The toil of it none may share;
혻혻혻혻By yourself must the way be won
혻혻혻혻Through fervid or frozen air
혻혻혻혻Till the overland journey占퐏 done;
혻혻혻혻And I would not take, for your own dear sake, one thorn from your track, my son.

혻혻혻혻Go forth to your hill and dale,
혻혻혻혻Yet take in your hand from me
혻혻혻혻A staff when your footsteps fail,
혻혻혻혻A weapon if need there be;
혻혻혻혻占폯will hum in your ear when the foeman占퐏 near, athirst for the victory.

혻혻혻혻In the desert of dusty death
혻혻혻혻It will point to the hidden spring;
혻혻혻혻Should you weary and fail for breath,
혻혻혻혻It will burgeon and branch and swing
혻혻혻혻Till you sink to sleep in its shadow deep to the sound of its murmuring.

혻혻혻혻.혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻 .혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻 .혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻 .혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻 .

혻혻혻혻You must face the general foe
혻혻혻혻A phantom pale and grim.
혻혻혻혻If you flinch at his glare, he占퐇l grow
혻혻혻혻And gather your strength to him;
혻혻혻혻But your power will rise if you laugh in his eyes and away in a mist he占퐇l swim.

혻혻혻혻To your freeborn soul be true
혻혻혻혻Fling parchment in the fire;
혻혻혻혻Men占퐏 laws are null for you,
혻혻혻혻For a word of Love is higher,
혻혻혻혻And can you do aught, when He rules your thought, but follow your own desire?

혻혻혻혻You will dread no pinching dearth
혻혻혻혻In the home where you love to lie,
혻혻혻혻For your floor will be good brown earth
혻혻혻혻And your roof the open sky.
혻혻혻혻There占퐇l be room for all at your festival when the heart-red wine runs high.

혻혻혻혻.혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻 .혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻 .혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻 .혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻 .

혻혻혻혻Joy to you, joy and strife
혻혻혻혻And a golden East before,
혻혻혻혻And the sound of the sea of life
혻혻혻혻In your ears when you reach the shore,
혻혻혻혻And a hope that still with as good a will you may fight as you fought of yore.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Le Gay Brereton, titled "Wilfred", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
